data.title: The yield on 10-year Japanese government bonds reached a new high since 1999.
data.description: On April 6th local time, the yield on newly issued 10-year Japanese government bonds, a benchmark for long-term interest rates, rose to 2.400%, the highest level since February 1999. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Rises Amid Trump's Threat to Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's recent threat to target Iran's energy facilities has led to a 1.3% increase in U.S. crude oil prices, reaching $113 per barrel at the market's opening. According to NS3.AI, Trump initially announced early Sunday that he would focus on Iran's power plants and bridges, setting a deadline for Tuesday at 8:00 PM Eastern Time. This follows a previous two-day ultimatum issued to Iran on March 21, which was later extended to April 6.

data.title: New Zealand Central Bank Expected to Maintain Interest Rates Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Moody's Analytics reported on April 6 that the Reserve Bank of New Zealand is expected to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2.25% this week. According to Jin10, the decision comes as the bank weighs inflation concerns against the impact of rising energy prices on the economy. The report suggests that the central bank's Monetary Policy Committee may signal patience, emphasizing that short-term inflation pressures can be temporarily overlooked as the economy gradually stabilizes.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Bitcoin may fall below $60,000 before rising to $250,000.
data.description: In a Coin Stories podcast, Arthur Hayes stated that he wouldn't put his last dollar into Bitcoin right now because the Federal Reserve hasn't been forced to expand liquidity. Hayes believes that tariff policies will lead to inflation and could prompt the US to move towards capital controls, which would be a huge catalyst for Bitcoin's liquidity. Hayes maintains his long-term price target for Bitcoin in this cycle between $250,000 and $750,000, but warns that if the US-Iran conflict continues, Bitcoin could fall below $60,000 in the short term. Furthermore, Charles Schwab confirmed that he will launch direct spot trading of Bitcoin and Ethereum through new accounts in the first half of 2026. Research from Mercado Bitcoin shows that in the 60 days following major global shocks, Bitcoin has consistently outperformed gold and the S&P 500. Currently, Bitcoin's price has rebounded to around $67,300, while the cryptocurrency fear and greed index has been in the extreme fear zone for several weeks.

data.title: Cryptocurrency lawyers say the Drift incident may constitute "civil negligence."
data.description: Attorney Ariel Givner stated that the $280 million attack on the Solana-based DeFi platform Drift Protocol could have been prevented through standard operating procedure security procedures, and this action may constitute civil negligence. The attack stemmed from the Drift team's failure to follow basic security procedures, including not storing signing keys in a physically isolated system and a lack of due diligence on blockchain developers at industry conferences. The Drift team stated in a post on the X platform that the attackers planned the attack for six months, initially contacting the team at a major crypto industry conference in October 2025. After establishing trust, the malicious actors hijacked developers' devices through malicious links and malware implants. Drift confirmed with medium-to-high confidence that the attackers were the same group involved in the October 2024 Radiant Capital attack. Class action lawsuit advertisements against Drift Protocol have begun circulating.

data.title: XRP ETF Inflows Decline Amid Weak Technical Momentum
data.description: XRP's spot ETF has experienced a significant drop in weekly net inflows, decreasing from approximately $200 million at its launch to less than $2 million recently. According to NS3.AI, this decline is attributed to weaker technical momentum, monthly escrow unlocks, and limited institutional participation. The analysis highlights that XRP continues to lag behind Bitcoin and Ethereum in terms of performance and investor interest.

data.title: Trump issues another ultimatum, oil prices rise.
data.description: Oil prices opened higher after US President Donald Trump threatened to strike Iranian energy facilities if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened. US crude rose 1.3% to $113 a barrel. "Tuesday will be Iran's power plant day and bridge day, all at once. Absolutely unprecedented!!! Open the Strait now," Trump tweeted Sunday morning. A few hours later, he appeared to set a new deadline: "Tuesday, 8:00 p.m. ET!" Trump had previously issued a similar two-day ultimatum to Iran on March 21, but later extended the deadline to April 6. (CNN)

data.title: Bahrain has called on the United Nations to pass a resolution to open the Strait of Hormuz by force.
data.description: The Bahraini Foreign Minister has called on the United Nations to adopt a resolution authorizing the use of force to open the Strait of Hormuz in order to avert a humanitarian and economic crisis. The Foreign Minister stated, "This draft resolution is not intended to escalate the situation, but rather to provide a framework for preventing further collapse of the international system by emphasizing the need to protect freedom of navigation and transit and to deter further attacks." (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: Kenneth Rogoff Predicts Yuan's Rise as Global Reserve Currency
data.description: Kenneth Rogoff has projected that the Chinese yuan could achieve the status of a global reserve currency within the next five years. According to NS3.AI, this prediction aligns with President Xi Jinping's efforts to promote the internationalization of the yuan. Rogoff also expressed criticism towards the US GENIUS Act, describing it as excessively liberal in its approach to stablecoin regulation.

data.title: Ethereum Surpasses 2100 USDT Mark with 1.7% Increase
data.description: Ethereum has surpassed the 2100 USDT mark, according to Odaily. The cryptocurrency is currently trading at 2101.17 USDT, reflecting a 24-hour increase of 1.7%.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Zhejiang University Research Enhances AI Learning with Human Brain Signals
data.description: A research team from Zhejiang University has introduced a novel approach to training deep neural networks using human brain signals. According to NS3.AI, this method has demonstrated an average improvement of 20.5% in few-shot learning and abstract concept recognition in new contexts. The study also revealed that while larger models enhance accuracy in concrete concepts, they tend to decrease accuracy in abstract concepts.

data.title: Bitcoin Futures Open with $590 Gap Reflecting Weekend Trading
data.description: CME Bitcoin futures began the new session with a notable gap of approximately $590. According to NS3.AI, this occurred after the contract concluded the previous session at $67,165 and reopened at $67,755. The gap highlights the impact of weekend spot market trading during CME's closure.

data.title: CME Reports Low Probability of Fed Rate Changes in April
data.description: On April 6, Jin10 reported that according to CME's 'FedWatch', the probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April stands at 1.6%, while the likelihood of maintaining the current rate is 98.4%. Furthermore, by June, the probability of a cumulative rate cut of 25 basis points is 1.8%, with a 96.6% chance of keeping rates unchanged, and a 1.5% probability of a cumulative rate hike of 25 basis points.

data.title: Murad Mahmudov Highlights SPX6900's Market Cap Stability
data.description: Murad Mahmudov has noted that SPX6900 is stabilizing around a market cap of $244 million. According to NS3.AI, he compared this stability to the pre-rally consolidation phases of Dogecoin and Pepe. Data from Arkham Intelligence reveals that Mahmudov continues to hold approximately 29.96 million SPX, valued at around $7.8 million, despite facing nearly $60 million in unrealized losses.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Gold Falls Below $4,640 per Ounce
data.description: Spot gold prices have dropped below $4,640 per ounce, marking a 0.80% decline within the day. According to Jin10, this decrease reflects ongoing market volatility and investor concerns over global economic conditions. The precious metal's price movement is closely watched by traders and analysts as they assess the potential impacts on investment strategies and market sentiment.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Futures Open Lower as Major Indices Decline
data.description: U.S. stock futures opened lower on Monday, with the three major indices experiencing declines. According to Jin10, Dow Jones Industrial Average futures fell by 0.38%, S&P 500 futures decreased by 0.31%, and Nasdaq futures dropped by 0.35%. The market's downward movement reflects investor caution amid ongoing economic uncertainties.
